title: Data error when fork-join_none and automatic variables are used in loop
body:
Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

```Verilog
`timescale 1ns/1ps
task automatic run();

    for (int i=0; i<5; i++) begin
        fork
            begin
                #1ns;
                $display("%s (%0d) @%0t: i=%0d", `__FILE__, `__LINE__, $time, i);
            end
        join_none
    end
    #2ns;
    for (int i=0; i<5; i++) begin
        automatic int ii = i;
        fork
            begin
                #1ns;
                $display("%s (%0d) @%0t: i=%0d", `__FILE__, `__LINE__, $time, ii);
            end
        join_none
    end
    #2ns;
endtask: run

module test();
    initial begin
        run();
        $finish;
    end
endmodule
```
expected results (from VCS)：
```shell
test.sv (8) @1000: i=5
test.sv (8) @1000: i=5
test.sv (8) @1000: i=5
test.sv (8) @1000: i=5
test.sv (8) @1000: i=5
test.sv (18) @3000: i=0
test.sv (18) @3000: i=1
test.sv (18) @3000: i=2
test.sv (18) @3000: i=3
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
```
Actual results:
```shell
test.sv (8) @1000: i=0
test.sv (8) @1000: i=1
test.sv (8) @1000: i=3
test.sv (8) @1000: i=4
test.sv (8) @1000: i=2
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
test.sv (18) @3000: i=4
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
Verilator 5.006 2023-01-22 rev UNKNOWN.REV 
(installed by brew on macOS )

What OS and distribution are you using?
macOS 13.4.1 on Apple Silicon M2

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I am a DV engineer with hardware background, instead of software background. Therefore I don't think I am able to fix it.
